name: Human Gate
description: Handles human-in-the-loop gating for trust enforcement. Sends fire-and-forget webhook POST notifications (AS008) to configured human_gate.webhook_url when gate triggers (FINANCIAL/AUTH/COMPLIANCE tier, LOW_TRUST_AUTHORITATIVE, unresolvable conflict). Request body includes node, finding type, trust score, data tier, required action. Webhook failure is non-fatal. block_on_gate config controls whether deploy is blocked pending approval. Actual approval flows back through reset-taint CLI/API — this component only handles the notification side. Also emits gate events to Stigmergy. Implemented as three composable layers: pure gate evaluation, isolated webhook notification, and orchestration.

types.name: GateTriggerReason
types.description: Reason a human gate was triggered. Maps to data classification tiers and trust/authority conditions.

types.name: FindingType
types.description: Type of finding that may trigger a gate. Subset of arbiter finding types relevant to gating.

types.name: DataTier
types.description: Data classification tier. Used in gate evaluation to determine if human review is required.

types.name: RequiredAction
types.description: Action required from the human reviewer before the gate can be cleared.

types.name: HumanGateConfig

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: Whether the human gate is active. When false, evaluate_gate still runs but trigger_human_gate short-circuits.

fields.name: webhook_url
fields.description: URL to POST gate notifications to. Must be a valid http:// or https:// URL when enabled is true. Empty string permitted only when enabled is false.

validators.error_message: webhook_url must be an http:// or https:// URL or empty string

fields.name: block_on_gate
fields.description: Whether deployments should be blocked pending human approval when a gate fires. This component sets the 'blocked' flag; enforcement is downstream.

fields.name: timeout_seconds
fields.description: HTTP timeout in seconds for webhook POST. Must be positive. Default matches cold-start target of 3 seconds.

validators.error_message: timeout_seconds must be between 0.1 and 30.0 seconds

fields.name: schema_version
fields.description: Schema version string included in webhook payload for consumer compatibility. Fixed to '1' for this contract version.

types.description: Configuration for the human gate component. Loaded from arbiter config YAML under human_gate key.

types.name: GateDecision

fields.name: triggered
fields.description: Whether the gate condition was met and human review is required.

fields.name: trigger_reason
fields.description: Reason the gate fired. None if triggered is false.

fields.name: required_action
fields.description: Action required from reviewer. None if triggered is false.

fields.name: summary
fields.description: Human-readable summary of the gate decision. Always includes node_id.

types.description: Pure result of gate evaluation. Frozen Pydantic model. Produced by evaluate_gate before any side effects.

types.name: OptionalGateTriggerReason
types.description: Optional GateTriggerReason — None when gate is not triggered.

types.name: OptionalRequiredAction
types.description: Optional RequiredAction — None when gate is not triggered.

types.name: GateEvent

fields.name: event_id
fields.description: Globally unique event identifier. UUID4 string.

validators.error_message: event_id must be a valid lowercase UUID4

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of gate event creation. Must end with +00:00 or Z.

validators.error_message: timestamp must be UTC (ending in Z or +00:00)

fields.name: node_id
fields.description: Identifier of the node that triggered the gate.

validators.error_message: node_id must be non-empty and at most 512 characters

fields.name: finding_type
fields.description: Type of finding that caused the gate to fire.

fields.name: trust_score
fields.description: Raw trust score of the node at time of gate evaluation.

validators.error_message: trust_score must be between 0.0 and 1.0 inclusive

fields.name: data_tier
fields.description: Data classification tier of the data involved in the finding.

fields.name: trigger_reason
fields.description: Why the gate was triggered.

fields.name: required_action
fields.description: What the human reviewer must do.

fields.name: summary
fields.description: Human-readable summary of the gate event. Includes node_id and trigger context.

fields.name: schema_version
fields.description: Schema version for webhook consumer compatibility. Matches HumanGateConfig.schema_version.

types.description: Frozen Pydantic model representing a gate event. Emitted to Stigmergy and sent via webhook. Serves as the canonical record of a gate firing.

types.name: WebhookResult

fields.name: success
fields.description: True if webhook POST returned a 2xx status code with no redirect.

fields.name: status_code
fields.description: HTTP status code returned, or None if the request failed before receiving a response.

fields.name: error_message
fields.description: Human-readable error description. None on success. Includes node_id and webhook_url context.

fields.name: response_body
fields.description: Response body on non-2xx responses for diagnostic logging. None on success or connection failure. Truncated to 1024 characters.

fields.name: elapsed_ms
fields.description: Time elapsed for the webhook request in milliseconds.

types.description: Frozen Pydantic model capturing the outcome of a webhook POST attempt. Never raises — all outcomes are captured in this model.

types.name: GateResult

fields.name: event_id
fields.description: UUID4 event identifier. Matches the GateEvent.event_id if gate was triggered, or a unique tracking ID if not.

validators.error_message: event_id must be a valid lowercase UUID4

fields.name: triggered
fields.description: Whether the gate was triggered and notifications were attempted.

fields.name: trigger_reason
fields.description: Reason the gate fired. None if not triggered.

fields.name: stigmergy_emitted
fields.description: Whether the gate event was successfully emitted to Stigmergy.

fields.name: notification_sent
fields.description: Whether the webhook notification was successfully delivered (2xx, no redirect).

fields.name: notification_error
fields.description: Error message from webhook attempt. None on success or if gate was not triggered.

fields.name: blocked
fields.description: Whether the deployment should be blocked pending human approval. True only when triggered=true AND config.block_on_gate=true.

types.description: Frozen Pydantic model capturing the full outcome of a human gate orchestration. Returned by trigger_human_gate.

types.name: StigmeryEmitter

fields.name: emit
fields.description: Callable signature: (event: GateEvent) -> bool. Represented as a string type reference for contract purposes. Implementation is a callable.

types.description: Callable dependency for emitting events to Stigmergy. Injected into trigger_human_gate for testability. In production, wraps stigmergy.emit_event. Accepts a GateEvent and returns bool indicating delivery success.

functions.name: evaluate_gate
functions.description: Pure function: evaluates whether a human gate should be triggered based on node findings, trust score, and data tier. Deterministic given the same inputs. No side effects. Returns a GateDecision with trigger_reason and required_action if triggered, or triggered=false with None fields otherwise. Gate fires for FINANCIAL/AUTH/COMPLIANCE data tiers, LOW_TRUST_AUTHORITATIVE conditions, or unresolvable conflicts.

inputs.name: node_id
inputs.description: Identifier of the node being evaluated.

validators.error_message: node_id must be non-empty and at most 512 characters

inputs.name: finding_type
inputs.description: Type of finding to evaluate for gate triggering.

inputs.name: trust_score
inputs.description: Raw trust score of the node. Used for LOW_TRUST_AUTHORITATIVE evaluation.

validators.error_message: trust_score must be between 0.0 and 1.0 inclusive

inputs.name: data_tier
inputs.description: Data classification tier of the data involved.

inputs.name: is_authoritative
inputs.description: Whether the node holds declared authority (from manifest). Required for LOW_TRUST_AUTHORITATIVE evaluation.

inputs.name: is_unresolvable_conflict
inputs.description: Whether the finding represents a conflict that cannot be automatically resolved.

error_cases.name: invalid_trust_score
error_cases.condition: trust_score is NaN, infinite, or outside [0.0, 1.0]

error_data.detail: trust_score must be a finite float between 0.0 and 1.0, got {value} for node {node_id}

error_cases.name: empty_node_id
error_cases.condition: node_id is empty string or only whitespace

error_data.detail: node_id must be non-empty for gate evaluation

preconditions: trust_score is a raw computed value from the trust ledger, not a display tier mapping
is_authoritative reflects declared authority from manifests, not earned trust
postconditions: If triggered is true, trigger_reason is not None
If triggered is true, required_action is not None
If triggered is false, trigger_reason is None and required_action is None
summary always contains node_id
Result is deterministic: same inputs always produce same output
FINANCIAL_TIER triggers when data_tier is FINANCIAL
AUTH_TIER triggers when data_tier is AUTH
COMPLIANCE_TIER triggers when data_tier is COMPLIANCE
LOW_TRUST_AUTHORITATIVE triggers when is_authoritative is true AND trust_score < 0.5
UNRESOLVABLE_CONFLICT triggers when is_unresolvable_conflict is true

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function with no side effects

functions.name: send_gate_notification
functions.description: Sends a webhook POST notification for a gate event. Uses stdlib urllib.request with configurable timeout. Never raises exceptions — all outcomes captured in WebhookResult. Includes X-Gate-Event-Id header and Content-Type: application/json. Does not follow HTTP redirects (3xx treated as failure). Logs non-2xx response bodies for diagnostics. Error messages always include node_id and webhook_url context.

inputs.name: event
inputs.description: The gate event to send as the webhook POST body (JSON-serialized).

inputs.name: config
inputs.description: Human gate configuration containing webhook_url and timeout_seconds.

error_data.detail: Webhook POST timed out after {timeout}s to {webhook_url} for node {node_id}

error_cases.name: dns_resolution_failure
error_cases.condition: Webhook URL hostname cannot be resolved

error_data.detail: DNS resolution failed for {webhook_url} for node {node_id}

error_cases.name: ssl_error
error_cases.condition: TLS handshake fails for https:// URL

error_data.detail: SSL error connecting to {webhook_url} for node {node_id}
Redirect received from {webhook_url} for node {node_id}, redirects are prohibited

error_cases.name: non_2xx_response
error_cases.condition: Server responds with non-2xx, non-3xx status

error_data.detail: Webhook POST to {webhook_url} returned {status_code} for node {node_id}

error_cases.name: serialization_error
error_cases.condition: GateEvent cannot be serialized to JSON (should not occur with valid Pydantic model)

error_data.detail: Failed to serialize gate event for node {node_id}

preconditions: config.webhook_url is a non-empty valid http:// or https:// URL
event is a valid frozen GateEvent with all required fields
postconditions: Function never raises — all error conditions produce a WebhookResult with success=false
On success (2xx, no redirect): success=true, status_code is set, error_message is None, response_body is None
On non-2xx response: success=false, status_code is set, response_body contains truncated body (max 1024 chars)
On redirect (3xx): success=false, error_message mentions redirect prohibition
On connection/timeout error: success=false, status_code is None, error_message describes the failure
error_message always includes event.node_id and config.webhook_url when present
elapsed_ms is always set and non-negative
Request includes X-Gate-Event-Id header with event.event_id
Request includes Content-Type: application/json header
Request body is JSON-serialized GateEvent including schema_version

structured_side_effects.description: HTTP POST to configured webhook URL with GateEvent JSON body, X-Gate-Event-Id header, and Content-Type: application/json
Logs webhook attempt, success/failure, elapsed time, and non-2xx response bodies at appropriate levels (INFO for success, WARNING for failure)

functions.name: trigger_human_gate
functions.description: Orchestrates the full human gate flow: evaluates gate condition, emits GateEvent to Stigmergy (system of record, first), then sends webhook notification (best-effort, second). Returns GateResult summarizing the entire operation. If config.enabled is false, short-circuits with triggered=false. If gate evaluates to not-triggered, short-circuits without Stigmergy emission or webhook. Stigmergy emitter is an injectable callable for testability. OpenTelemetry span wraps the full orchestration.

inputs.name: node_id
inputs.description: Identifier of the node being evaluated.

validators.error_message: node_id must be non-empty and at most 512 characters

inputs.name: finding_type
inputs.description: Type of finding to evaluate.

inputs.name: trust_score
inputs.description: Raw trust score of the node.

validators.error_message: trust_score must be between 0.0 and 1.0 inclusive

inputs.name: data_tier
inputs.description: Data classification tier.

inputs.name: is_authoritative
inputs.description: Whether the node holds declared authority.

inputs.name: is_unresolvable_conflict
inputs.description: Whether the finding is an unresolvable conflict.

inputs.name: config
inputs.description: Human gate configuration.

inputs.name: stigmergy_emitter
inputs.description: Injectable callable: (GateEvent) -> bool. Emits gate event to Stigmergy. Returns true on successful emission. In production wraps stigmergy.emit_event; in tests use a mock/stub.

error_cases.name: invalid_trust_score
error_cases.condition: trust_score is NaN, infinite, or outside [0.0, 1.0]

error_data.detail: trust_score must be a finite float between 0.0 and 1.0, got {value} for node {node_id}

error_cases.name: empty_node_id
error_cases.condition: node_id is empty string or only whitespace

error_data.detail: node_id must be non-empty for gate evaluation

error_cases.name: config_enabled_no_webhook_url
error_cases.condition: config.enabled is true but config.webhook_url is empty

error_data.detail: HumanGateConfig.webhook_url must be set when enabled is true for node {node_id}
Stigmergy emission failed for node {node_id}, continuing with webhook. Error: {error}

preconditions: config is a valid HumanGateConfig (if enabled, webhook_url is non-empty)
stigmergy_emitter is a callable accepting GateEvent and returning bool
trust_score is a raw computed value, not a display tier
postconditions: If config.enabled is false: triggered=false, stigmergy_emitted=false, notification_sent=false, blocked=false
If evaluate_gate returns triggered=false: triggered=false, stigmergy_emitted=false, notification_sent=false, blocked=false
If triggered: Stigmergy emission is attempted before webhook notification
If triggered and Stigmergy emission fails: webhook is still attempted (best-effort both)
blocked is true only when triggered=true AND config.block_on_gate=true
event_id is always a valid UUID4
GateResult.trigger_reason matches GateDecision.trigger_reason when triggered
notification_error is None when notification_sent is true or when not triggered

structured_side_effects.description: Emits GateEvent to Stigmergy via injected stigmergy_emitter callable. Attempted first as system of record.
HTTP POST webhook notification via send_gate_notification. Attempted second, best-effort.
Logs gate evaluation result, Stigmergy emission status, webhook status, and overall gate result. INFO for normal flow, WARNING for failures, DEBUG for short-circuit paths.

functions.name: build_gate_event
functions.description: Constructs a frozen GateEvent Pydantic model from a GateDecision and input context. Generates a UUID4 event_id and UTC timestamp. Pure factory function except for UUID generation and clock read.

inputs.name: node_id
inputs.description: Node identifier.

inputs.name: finding_type
inputs.description: Finding type from the evaluation context.

inputs.name: trust_score
inputs.description: Raw trust score.

inputs.name: data_tier
inputs.description: Data classification tier.

inputs.name: decision
inputs.description: The gate decision from evaluate_gate. Must have triggered=true.

inputs.name: schema_version
inputs.description: Schema version to include in the event.

error_data.detail: Cannot build GateEvent from non-triggered decision for node {node_id}

postconditions: Returned GateEvent.event_id is a valid UUID4
Returned GateEvent.timestamp is UTC ISO 8601
Returned GateEvent.node_id matches input node_id
Returned GateEvent.trigger_reason matches decision.trigger_reason
Returned GateEvent.required_action matches decision.required_action
Returned GateEvent.schema_version matches input schema_version
Returned GateEvent is a frozen Pydantic model

structured_side_effects.description: Near-pure factory: only side effects are UUID4 generation and UTC clock read

functions.name: validate_human_gate_config
functions.description: Validates a HumanGateConfig for internal consistency. Checks that webhook_url is set when enabled, timeout_seconds is within bounds, and schema_version is recognized. Returns list of validation error strings (empty list means valid).

inputs.name: config
inputs.description: Configuration to validate.

postconditions: Returns empty list if config is valid
Returns list of human-readable error strings if config is invalid
Checks: enabled=true requires non-empty webhook_url
Checks: webhook_url must match http:// or https:// pattern when non-empty
Checks: timeout_seconds must be in [0.1, 30.0]
Checks: schema_version must be '1' for this contract version

structured_side_effects.description: Pure validation function with no side effects

invariants: All GateEvent timestamps are UTC (timezone-aware, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
GateEvent and GateDecision and GateResult and WebhookResult are frozen Pydantic models — never mutated after construction
Trust score is raw float, never mapped to display tiers for policy decisions
Stigmergy emission always precedes webhook notification in orchestration — Stigmergy is system of record
Webhook failure is always non-fatal — never raises, never blocks orchestration
All error messages include the specific node_id and, where applicable, webhook_url that caused the error
The trust ledger is never written to or read from by this component — gate decisions are based on supplied trust_score
Trust and authority are distinct: LOW_TRUST_AUTHORITATIVE trigger fires only when trust is low AND authority is declared (never conflated)
schema_version in webhook payload is always '1' for this contract version
HTTP redirects are never followed on webhook POST — a redirect is treated as a webhook failure
event_id is globally unique per gate evaluation (UUID4)
send_gate_notification uses stdlib urllib.request only — no third-party HTTP clients
block_on_gate controls the 'blocked' field in GateResult but does not actually block execution — downstream consumers enforce blocking
